layman_headline: Market fear is high but no clean setup stands out — patience is the play right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin dominance squeezing altcoins. Most candidates are either in confirmed downtrends with no reversal signals yet, or have already bounced sharply and may be overextended. Ethereum has a low RSI near oversold and is the strongest large-cap option, but its chart structure is still bearish with price below all moving averages. Jumping in now without a clearer reversal signal risks catching a falling knife.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. If Ethereum pulls back to the $1,750-$1,780 zone and holds support with a bullish daily candle, that could be a much better entry. Don't force trades in a fearful, BTC-dominant market.
expert_reasoning: Evaluating all 10 candidates: BONK is in freefall with a bearish Marubozu and -1.40 chart bias — too dangerous despite oversold RSI. LIT has RSI at 70 (overbought) after a 53% 5-day run — chasing. HYPE has the strongest chart bias (+1.10) but RSI at 59 isn't compelling and the 1:1 R/R ratio is poor. LDO surged 24% in 7 days and is likely extended despite the 6.5x R/R ratio. ETH at RSI 35 is the most promising given our 80% win rate on RSI signals, but price is below all MAs with a neutral chart bias and poor 0.36x R/R to nearest levels. The Fear & Greed at 27 is improving from 25, which is a mild positive, but BTC dominance at 56.5% creates strong headwinds for altcoins. No candidate offers a compelling risk/reward with confirmed reversal. Best strategy is to wait for ETH to test $1,700-$1,780 support and form a higher low.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% continues draining capital from altcoins — could persist for weeks
Iran strikes creating geopolitical risk that could push Fear & Greed deeper into extreme fear
ETH is below all moving averages — bearish structure could lead to further breakdown below $1,700
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,700-$1,780 support zone — a bounce with volume confirmation would be actionable
Fear & Greed index trajectory — if it drops below 20 (extreme fear), contrarian buy setups become stronger
BTC dominance — any reversal below 55% would signal capital rotating back into alts
market_context: The market is in a fear-driven environment with BTC flat and altcoins bleeding. Geopolitical tensions from Iran strikes and high BTC dominance at 56.5% create unfavorable conditions for alt swing trades.
context_banner: Fear-driven market with BTC dominance crushing alts — discipline demands waiting for confirmed reversal setups rather than catching knives.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but needs chart confirmation before acting.
Fear & Greed at 27 improving from 25 suggests sentiment may be bottoming, but hasn't reached extreme fear levels that produce the best contrarian entries.
Bitcoin price sagging under $62.5K amid Iran strikes adds geopolitical risk that could push the entire market lower before any recovery.
ETH derivatives reset coverage suggests positioning is cleaning up, which could set the stage for a stronger move once the reset completes.
HYPE has the strongest chart bias at +1.10 but all other candidates show neutral or bearish chart structures — no high-conviction bullish setup exists right now.
